source.sourcePath: raw/sources/DHF (인허가)/06_(Retrofit) HnX-R1/05_설계개발 검증 및 유효성 확인/08_Cybersecurity/3.1_A-CAV-HNX_Architecture View.docx

sourceSummary.oneLine: Cybersecurity architecture view for the Digital X-ray Imaging System, model HnX-R1.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Shows a cyber security architecture view for HnX-R1, including FDA-related global system, updateability/patchability, and security use case views.
keyPoints: The document scope is limited to HnX-R1, the Digital X-ray Imaging System.
Three of four architectural views corresponding to FDA requirements are included: Global system view, Updateability/Patchability view, and Security use case view.
The detector update process uses the standard communication procedure, receives a *.bpd update file, decrypts patch data, and performs a SHA-256 integrity check.
Security use cases identify risks using Data-Flow Diagram assets, STRIDE categories, cybersecurity controls, and IEC 60601-4-5 cybersecurity requirements.
The document refers to penetration test report no. EBB-2025-000109 for evaluation results.

evidence.claim: The purpose of the document is to show a cybersecurity architecture view for the Digital X-ray Imaging System model HnX-R1.
evidence.evidenceText: The purpose of this document is to show a cyber security architecture view for Digital X-ray Imaging System (Model name: HnX-R1).

evidence.claim: The scope of the document is limited to HnX-R1.
evidence.evidenceText: This document is limited to HnX-R1, the Digital X-ray Imaging System.

evidence.claim: The document includes three FDA-related architectural views and excludes the multi patient harm view.
evidence.evidenceText: three of the four architectural views corresponding to the FDA requirements are Global system view, Updateability/Patchability view, and Security use case view. Multi patient harm view is excluded because it is not our product.

evidence.claim: The detector update process proceeds only after a proper standard communication connection and uses no additional ports.
evidence.evidenceText: The detector's update process can only proceed after a proper connection has been established through the standard communication procedure. No additional ports are used.

evidence.claim: The console sends a *.bpd update file to the detector and initiates the patch request.
evidence.evidenceText: The console sends the update file (*.bpd) to the detector and initiates the patch request.

evidence.claim: The detector decrypts received patch data and performs a SHA-256 integrity check.
evidence.evidenceText: The detector decrypts the received patch data and performs a SHA-256 integrity check.

evidence.claim: Security risks are identified according to Data-Flow Diagram assets using STRIDE threat categories.

reviewFlags.reason: Manufacturer or legal entity name is not present in the source text.
Authority is inferred from the source statement about FDA requirements and source metadata authoritySignal; the document is not itself an FDA publication.
Figures referenced as Systematic Functional Diagram and Data Flow Diagram are present only as placeholders in the preprocessed text, so diagram contents may be incomplete.
